[וע"ד שנתבאר לעיל (סעיף ז) בענין גילוי כח הא"ס שבהולדה, שהגילוי דא"ס הוא בענין מוגבל]. ומזה מובן, דהגם שגילוי היחידה הכללית דלע"ל הוא ע"י גילוי היחידה עכשיו, מ"מ, גילוי היחידה עכשיו לגבי גילוי היחידה הכללית דלע"ל הוא באין ערוך. ועד"ז הוא בנוגע לפדה בשלום נפשי שהי' אצל דוד, דכיון שהפדי' בשלום שלו היתה רק בנוגע לכמה אנשים (עמשי, אנשי אבשלום וכו') ולא בכללות העולם (דבכללות העולם היו מנגדים), לכן, הוא באין ערוך כלל לגבי הפדי' בשלום דלע"ל, שלא יהיו בה הגבלות. והמשכת הפדי' בשלום דלע"ל (בלי גבול) ע"י הפדי' דשלום דדוד (שהיא בנוגע לענינים מוגבלים) הוא ע"ד המשכת ענין ההילוך והבלי גבול ע"י קיום התומ"צ בעוה"ז באלו העוסקים רק בנגלה דתורה. והענין דפדה בשלום נפשי שבהגאולה דיו"ד כסלו [ועד"ז בהגאולה די"ט כסלו ודי"ב תמוז], כיון שהגאולה היא בנוגע לפנימיות התורה, היחידה דתורה[ftnref\_566\_60], לכן, הענין דפדה בשלום נפשי שבגאולה זו, אין בו מדידות והגבלות, והוא בנוגע לכל הענינים.
[cup]וזהו[/cup] שיום התשיעי בכסלו, יום ההילולא, הוא הערב דיום הגאולה[ftnref\_567\_61], כי ע"י שהגילוי דפדה בשלום נפשי הי' לאחרי קדימת העבודה דהילולא של בעל הגאולה, ענין הביטול (שבדומה להסתלקות), המשיך גילוי זה (הגילוי דפדה בשלום נפשי שלמעלה ממדידה והגבלה) גם *בהמציאות* דכל השייכים אליו. שלכן, כדי לקבל גילוי זה, אין צריכה להיות הכנה דביטול ויציאה מהמציאות, ואין הדבר תלוי אלא ברצון, וביכלתו של כל אחד (גם לפני שיצא ממצבו) לפדות את עצמו באופן דפדה *בשלום* נפשי, שלום בתכלית השלימות, ועי"ז לפדות גם את כל העולם, בגאולה האמיתית והשלימה ע"י משיח צדקנו, במהרה בימינו ממש ובעגלא דידן ממש.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**English:**

## With the help of Hashem. Shabbos Parshas Vayeitzei, 10 Kislev, 5746

**"He has redeemed my soul in peace from those who draw near to me, for there were many with me."** It is well known that beyond the simple meaning of this verse—that Dovid spoke regarding his own redemption, that Hashem redeemed him from the wars that came upon him—this verse also alludes to the redemption of several Tzaddikim, and certainly to the redemption of the Tzaddikim who recited this verse at the time they were redeemed. It is worth noting that in all three of the redemptions of our Rebbes—the redemption of the Alter Rebbe on 19 Kislev, the redemption of the Mitteler Rebbe on 10 Kislev, and the redemption of the Rebbe Rayatz on 12 Tammuz—the masters of the redemption recited this verse on the day they were redeemed. Regarding the Alter Rebbe’s redemption on 19 Kislev, it is known what the Alter Rebbe wrote in his Iggeres HaKodesh: "When I read in the Book of Tehillim the verse, 'He has redeemed my soul in peace,' etc., I emerged in peace from the 'Hashem of Peace.'" Regarding the Mitteler Rebbe’s redemption on 10 Kislev, it is known that our Rebbes would recite the daily portion of Tehillim as divided by the days of the month; since the verse "He has redeemed my soul" is in the monthly Tehillim portion for the tenth day of the month, he recited this verse on the day of his redemption (the tenth of Kislev). And regarding the redemption of the Rebbe Rayatz on 12 Tammuz, it is known that it was the custom of our Rebbes that at special times and situations, they would add the recitation of the Tehillim portion as divided by the days of the week (in addition to the monthly portion). Since the calendar placement of 12 Tammuz in the year 5687 (the year of the imprisonment and redemption) was on the third day of the week, and the verse "He has redeemed my soul" is in the weekly Tehillim portion for the third day, it is understood that the master of the redemption of 12 Tammuz recited this verse on the day of his redemption.

It is explained elsewhere that the connection of the 10 Kislev redemption to the verse "He has redeemed my soul in peace" is (in one respect) even greater than the connection of the 19 Kislev and 12 Tammuz redemptions to this verse, for since this verse is in the Tehillim portion for the tenth day of the month, the connection of this verse to the redemption of 10 Kislev is emphasized every single year.

**b)** It is known how the master of the redemption explains in his Maamar (printed in his sefer *Shaarei Teshuvah*) that the emphasis in the verse "He has redeemed my soul in peace" is (in addition to the matter of redemption itself) on the *manner* of the redemption—that the redemption is in peace, without any war whatsoever. He explains there that the true essence of peace is that one has no adversary at all. This is similar to how it was in the days of Shlomo, when all the nations were *batel* (nullified) to him. Seemingly, the intention is that as long as one has adversaries, even if they are not actually fighting him (because his awe and fear have fallen upon them, etc.), this is not the true essence of peace. For when there are adversaries, there is a possibility and a place for war, whereas the true essence of peace is (not only that there is no actual war, but) that there is (also) no possibility or place for war.

This is why the matter of peace existed specifically in the days of Shlomo—which is why he was called Shlomo, because there was peace (*shalom*) in his days—for all the nations *became batel* to Shlomo (due to the intensity of his greatness). When the adversary is nullified (at which point there is no place for him to fight), that is the true essence of peace. This is what Hashem said to King Dovid (when he wanted to build the Beis HaMikdash): "You shall not build a house for My name, for you are a man of war," even though at that time (when Dovid wanted to build the Beis HaMikdash) he was in a state of rest, and Hashem had given him rest from all his enemies round about. This is because Dovid’s rest came about because he had conquered all the wars, not because his enemies had become *batel*. Since adversaries still remained in his days (and there was a place for them to fight), this was not the true essence of rest and peace. This is the meaning of "He has redeemed my soul in peace"—the matter of redemption in peace is that the adversary is nullified, as it was in the days of Shlomo.

The Maamar continues that the true essence of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" is that those who were previously enemies and adversaries become lovers. This matter is even higher than the peace that existed in the days of Shlomo, for the peace in the days of Shlomo was that everyone became *batel* due to the revelation of light (but they were not transformed into holiness), whereas the true essence of peace (that even the adversary is transformed into holiness) will be in the future, as it is written: "For then I will turn to the peoples a clear language, etc., to serve Him with one shoulder." And as for the fact that *Dovid* said "He has redeemed my soul *in peace*" (even though in his days the adversaries were not nullified, and certainly not transformed into holiness, as this did not exist even in the days of Shlomo and will only be in the future), it is explained in the Maamar of the master of the redemption that this refers to the soul of Dovid as it will be in the future.

According to what is known regarding the interpretation of the Sages' statement that "one is judged by his own words" (and similarly regarding the interpretation of the Mishnah’s language of "judgment and accounting"), when a person issues a verdict for another person, he is thereby issuing a verdict for himself. Thus, the verdict of the Mitteler Rebbe in his Maamar *Podeh B'Shalom Nafshi*—that the meaning of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" is that the adversary is nullified (like the peace in the days of Shlomo), and furthermore, that the adversary is transformed into a lover (like the peace that will be in the future)—is a verdict also regarding the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" in his own redemption, the redemption of 10 Kislev. (From this, it is also understood regarding the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" in the redemption of 19 Kislev and the redemption of 12 Tammuz, that the peace in these redemptions is that the adversary is nullified, and furthermore, that he is transformed into a lover.)

**c)** The simple interpretation of the verse "He has redeemed my soul in peace" is that Dovid said this regarding his own redemption as it exists in this world, that *this* redemption was in peace. One must understand the connection and relevance of the interpretation in the Maamar—that Dovid’s statement, "He has redeemed my soul in peace," refers to the soul of Dovid as it will be in the future—with the simple interpretation that Dovid’s redemption now was a redemption in peace. The explanation for this can be said based on what the Rambam writes: that the section of Bilaam speaks of the two Mashiachs—the first Mashiach, who is Dovid, and the final Mashiach, who will arise from his descendants. It can be said that the reason Dovid is called the first Mashiach is because all the matters that will be brought about by King Mashiach (the final Mashiach) had their beginning through Dovid, the first Mashiach. However, their completion and perfection will be through the final Mashiach who arises from the descendants of Dovid. Based on this, we can explain the connection between the two interpretations of the verse "He has redeemed my soul in peace": that in their inner essence, Dovid’s redemption now was also a redemption in peace—meaning that the adversaries were *nullified* (and not merely that they did not fight him), and furthermore, that they were transformed into lovers. However, in Dovid, this was in an internal and hidden manner, and in the future, the inner essence of the redemption of Dovid’s soul will be revealed, which is a redemption in peace.

Based on this, the greatness of the elevation of the 10 Kislev redemption is understood even more, for the fact that it is described as a redemption in peace (without the added explanation that it is a preparation for the redemption in peace of the future, etc.) proves that the matter of peace within it [that the adversary is nullified, and furthermore, that he is transformed into a lover] is (also) in a revealed state.

**d)** It is known that Shabbos and Yom Tov require preparation on the day before them—Erev Shabbos and Erev Yom Tov. Regarding our matter, the festival of the redemption of 10 Kislev—its "Erev," the day that serves as the preparation for 10 Kislev, is the ninth of Kislev, the birthday and Yahrtzeit of the master of the redemption. Even though the redemption of 10 Kislev occurred in the year 5687, a year before his passing, nevertheless, since "merit is brought about through a meritorious day," it can be said that the fact that the ninth of Kislev merited to be the Yahrtzeit of the Mitteler Rebbe is because even before that, it was connected to this. Based on this, it can be said that the greatness of the elevation of the day of redemption [10 Kislev], where the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" is similar to the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" that will be in the future, is because its "Erev" is the Yahrtzeit of the master of the redemption.

**e)** This can be understood by prefacing what the master of the redemption writes in his Maamar *Lehavin Inyan Hilula d'Rashbi*: that the reason the day of the passing of the Rashbi is called a "Hilula" (wedding celebration) is because through the Rashbi, there was the revelation of the inner dimension of the Torah. He explains this by prefacing the Sages' statement: "This world, from which we depart, is like a wedding celebration." Through the descent of the souls below into this world—*ha'alma*—they undergo the process of *azlinan*, "we are walking/moving." Souls, as they are above, are called "standing," as it is written: "As Hashem, the G-d of Israel, lives, before whom I have stood." And in the Zohar it is stated: "Every single soul was *standing* in its form before the Holy King." Although souls, as they are above, are always in a state of *ratzo v'shov* (running and returning), through which they ascend from level to level, nevertheless, since all their ascents are within limitation—for even the highest levels (where the souls ascend) are according to the "measuring line"—they are therefore called "standing." Through the descent of the souls below, they become "walking," for the ascent of the souls through their descent below is in a state of *Ein Sof* and without limitation. This is the meaning of "this world, from which we depart"—the souls, as they are from their own perspective, are standing, and through their descent into this world, they become walking, *azlinan*.

The reason that it is specifically through this world that the matter of *azlinan* is achieved is because this world is like a wedding celebration. The matter is that the soul and the body are like a man and a woman. Just as with a man and a woman in the literal sense, for the union of male and female (who are two opposites) to occur, it is through the drawing down of the power of the *Ein Sof* that is above both of them—and this power that is drawn into them is revealed in the matter of procreation [for the matter of bringing forth generation after generation without limit is not from the nature of created beings, who are limited, but from the power of the *Ein Sof* that is above the nature of created beings, which is drawn down through marriage]—so too is it with the soul and the body, which are like a man and a woman. For the connection and union of the soul and body, which are two opposites, to be possible, it is through the drawing down of the power of the *Ein Sof*. And the drawing down of this power is revealed in the matter of procreation—"the offspring of the righteous are good deeds"—for the ascent achieved through Torah and Mitzvos is an ascent in a state of *Ein Sof* and without limitation. This is "this world, from which we depart, is like a wedding celebration"—the reason that specifically from it (this world) comes *azlinan* (we become walking) is because this world is like a wedding celebration, where the connection and union of the spiritual soul with the physical body (this world) is like the union of a bridegroom and bride (Hilula, a wedding), through which the power of the *Ein Sof* is drawn down.

**f)** It is known that just as the descent of the soul below is a descent for the sake of an ascent, so too is it with Torah and Mitzvos; the intention in the descent of Torah and Mitzvos is for the sake of an ascent. For through their descent below and their enclothement in physical things, the essential power of Torah and Mitzvos is drawn into them. Based on this, it can be said that the matter of Hilula in the performance of Torah and Mitzvos in this world specifically also applies to the Torah and Mitzvos themselves. In addition to the matter of Hilula in the person engaged in Torah and Mitzvos—the drawing down of the power of the *Ein Sof* through the connection and union of soul and body (as in section e)—there is also the matter of Hilula in the Torah and Mitzvos themselves. For Torah and Mitzvos are the wisdom and will of Hashem, which are above the world, and their connection with the matters of this world (the physical things in which the Torah and Mitzvos are enclothed) is a connection of two opposites. This connection is through the drawing down of the essential power of Torah and Mitzvos—similar to the union of bridegroom and bride, through the drawing down of the power of the *Ein Sof* that is above both of them and connects them.

**g)** The Maamar continues with another interpretation of "this world, from which we depart": that at the time of a person’s passing, when the soul departs from the body and from this world—*azlinan minei*—then the matter of *azlinan* is achieved within it, that it becomes in a state of "walking." According to the explanation above (section e), the soul becoming "walking" is while it is below in this world. And the interpretation of *azlinan minei* is "because of it and by its cause"—that the fact that the soul comes to the state of *azlinan*, that it becomes "walking," is *minei*, from the elevation of this world, which is like a wedding celebration. According to the second interpretation, the matter of *azlinan* that is achieved in the soul (that it becomes "walking") is at the time of *azlinan minei*, when it goes and departs from this world. The core of the explanation in the Maamar is that the Mitzvos, as they are below, are in a state of measurement and limitation. And the Torah of below is also in a state of measurement and limitation, as it is written: "Its measure is longer than the earth"—meaning that there is a measure to the Torah, but the measure is longer than the earth. Therefore, the "walking" (the matter of being without limit) that is achieved in the soul through its engagement in Torah and Mitzvos in this world is in a state of concealment. Also, the "walking" of the soul through the Mitzvah of procreation, even though in the matter of procreation there is a revelation of the power of the *Ein Sof*, nevertheless, since the revelation of the power of the *Ein Sof* in this is regarding a limited matter (the birth of children, albeit that the birth is generation after generation without limit), therefore, even through the performance of this Mitzvah, the matter of "walking" is in a state of concealment. After the soul fulfills its mission in this world and the time of *azlinan minei* arrives, when it goes (and departs) from the limitations of this world, then the inner essence of the Torah and Mitzvos in which it engaged in this world is revealed to it—that the inner essence of Torah and Mitzvos is without limit—and then the matter of *azlinan* (walking) is achieved within it in a revealed state as well. This is why the day of the passing of Tzaddikim who engaged in the inner dimension of the Torah is called a "Hilula" day (like the Hilula of the Rashbi, and similarly regarding the day of passing of all Tzaddikim who engaged in the inner dimension of the Torah), for through the study and engagement in the inner dimension of the Torah, the inner essence and the "without limit"—the matter of Hilula and walking—is revealed on the day of passing.

**h)** The master of the redemption explains in the aforementioned Maamar that in a Hilula (the union of male and female) there are three matters (in general): The union of male and female above. The matter of male and female also exists in souls as they are above before their descent below; there are souls that are in the category of male and souls that are in the category of female. The union of the souls that are in the category of male with the souls that are in the category of female is called a Hilula. And the union of male and female below. In this itself, there are two matters: the union of male and female in the literal sense, and the union of the soul with the body [which are like a man and a woman]. The third matter in a Hilula is the union of the soul (male) with the body (female) that will be at the resurrection of the dead. This Hilula is the highest level, for then there will be a revelation of the Essence.

The revelation that will be at the resurrection of the dead is (as explained in many places, and also in the aforementioned Maamar) that then the drawing down of the Essence that was achieved through the performance of Mitzvos in this world will be revealed. This is similar to what was explained above (section g) regarding the matter of the Hilula at the passing of Tzaddikim who engaged in the inner dimension of the Torah, which is the revelation of the inner essence and the "without limit" of the Torah and Mitzvos in which they engaged in this world. However, now, in order to receive the revelation, it is through the departure of the soul from the body, whereas in the future, this revelation will be even in the physical body (and the revelation itself will be higher, for then there will be a revelation of the Essence, as mentioned above).

It can be said that one of the reasons the matter of the third Hilula is explained in the discourse *Lehavin Inyan Hilula d'Rashbi* is to hint that the merit of the Tzaddikim whose primary engagement is to reveal the inner dimension of the Torah is in two matters: that their day of passing is called a "Hilula" day because then the inner essence and the "without limit" in the Torah and Mitzvos are revealed, as explained at length above. And also, that through their work to reveal the inner dimension of the Torah, the "without limit" of the Torah and Mitzvos is revealed [to them, and through them it is revealed somewhat also to all those connected to them] even in the soul as it is enclothed in the physical body, and also in the physical body itself, *similar* to how it will be at the resurrection of the dead. It should be added that the revelation of both matters, including the matter of the Hilula of passing, is in the soul as it is enclothed in the body. However, initially, the revelation of the "without limit" to the person is through his being in a state of *bitul* (nullification), where he exits his own existence—similar to the matter of passing—and through this, the "without limit" is subsequently revealed also *within the existence* of the person—similar to the revelation that will be at the resurrection of the dead.

**i)** From the above, it is understood that in the aforementioned "walking" (ascent in a state of *Ein Sof* and without limit) that is achieved through the performance of Torah and Mitzvos in this world, there are three levels. In souls that engage only in the revealed dimension of the Torah, the "walking" is in a state of concealment. For from the perspective of the external dimension (the revealed dimension of the Torah), the Torah and Mitzvos are in a state of measurement and limitation. And the revelation of the "without limit" that is drawn down through the performance of Torah and Mitzvos in this world will be primarily in the future. In souls that engage in the inner dimension of the Torah, the revelation of the "without limit" of the Torah and Mitzvos (the matter of "walking") is also at the time of their passing, and similarly while they are below, through the movement of *bitul* and exiting their own existence. Through this, they subsequently arrive at a higher level, where the revelation of the "without limit" of the Torah and Mitzvos is also within their own existence.

It can be said that it is similar regarding the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace," that it has (similar to) these three levels. The matter is that the true essence of "He has redeemed my soul in peace," that the adversary is nullified completely, as explained above (section b) from the Maamar of the master of the redemption, is through the revelation of the *Yechidah*. For the level of *Chayah*, since there is an "other side" (*sitra achra*) opposing it, its revelation does not negate and nullify completely the foreign desires of the animal soul; it is only that the desire for G-dliness (of the level of *Chayah*) overcomes the desires of the animal soul. For there to be a nullification of the desires of the animal soul, it is through the revelation of the *Yechidah*. Since the level of the *Yechidah* has no "other side" opposing it—"in its shadow, they have no power"—therefore, through the revelation of the *Yechidah*, the "other side" is nullified automatically.

This is why the true essence of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" will be with the coming of Mashiach specifically. For just as in a person, the nullification of the "other side" within him is through the revelation of the *Yechidah* specifically, so too is it in the world at large—that specifically through Mashiach, who is the *Yechidah* of the entire *Seder Hishtalshelus*, there will be a complete nullification of the *Sitra Achra*. However, since all the revelations of the future depend on our deeds and our work now, the matter of the nullification of the *Sitra Achra* through the revelation of the general *Yechidah* that will be in the future is also through the person revealing the *Yechidah* within himself, through which the foreign desires of his animal soul are nullified. In particular, when through the revelation of his *Yechidah* he also brings about (to a degree) the nullification of the *Sitra Achra* in others. Just as King Dovid, through his work, brought about that even Amasa said to him, "Peace, peace to you and peace to your helpers," and that even the men of Avshalom prayed for Dovid’s victory.

Since now all matters are in a state of limitation, therefore, the matter of *mesiras nefesh* that comes from the revelation of the *Yechidah* is also regarding a limited matter. [Similar to what was explained above (section g) regarding the revelation of the power of the *Ein Sof* in procreation, that the revelation of the *Ein Sof* is in a limited matter.] From this it is understood that although the revelation of the general *Yechidah* of the future is through the revelation of the *Yechidah* now, nevertheless, the revelation of the *Yechidah* now is incomparable to the revelation of the general *Yechidah* of the future. Similarly, regarding "He has redeemed my soul in peace" that Dovid experienced: since his redemption in peace was only regarding several people (Amasa, the men of Avshalom, etc.) and not in the world at large (for in the world at large there were adversaries), it is therefore incomparable to the redemption in peace of the future, which will have no limitations. The drawing down of the redemption in peace of the future (without limit) through the redemption in peace of Dovid (which is regarding limited matters) is similar to the drawing down of the matter of "walking" and the "without limit" through the performance of Torah and Mitzvos in this world by those who engage only in the revealed dimension of the Torah. And the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" in the redemption of 10 Kislev [and similarly in the redemption of 19 Kislev and 12 Tammuz], since the redemption is regarding the inner dimension of the Torah—the *Yechidah* of the Torah—therefore, the matter of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" in this redemption has no measurements or limitations, and it is regarding all matters.

This is why the ninth of Kislev, the Yahrtzeit day, is the "Erev" of the day of redemption, for through the fact that the revelation of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" occurred after the preceding work of the Hilula of the master of the redemption—the matter of *bitul* (which is similar to passing)—it drew down this revelation (the revelation of "He has redeemed my soul in peace" that is above measurement and limitation) even *within the existence* of all those connected to him. Therefore, in order to receive this revelation, there is no need for a preparation of *bitul* and exiting one's existence; the matter depends only on the will and the ability of each person (even before he has exited his own state) to redeem himself in the manner of "He has redeemed my soul *in peace*," peace in the ultimate perfection, and through this, to redeem the entire world as well, in the true and complete redemption through Mashiach Tzidkeinu, speedily and truly in our days, and in our very midst.

---

**Hebrew:**

## בס"ד. אור לי"ד כסלו ה'תשל"ט

### בס"ד. אור לי"ד כסלו ה'תשל"ט[ftnref\_568\_\*]

[cup]אשר[/cup] ברא ששון ושמחה חתן וכלה גילה רינה דיצה וחדוה אהבה ואחוה שלום וריעות[ftnref\_569\_1], ומביא כ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר במאמרו ד"ה זה[ftnref\_570\_2] (שבקונטרס דרושי חתונה תרפ"ט[ftnref\_571\_3]) את מאמר הזהר בפרשת תרומה[ftnref\_572\_4] שמבאר שם כל השבע ברכות בפרטיות, ואומר שם בנוגע לברכה השביעית [שהיא כוללת את כל הברכות], שביעאה איהו מקיים כולא, ומהאי שביעאה מתברכאן כולא ודאי כללא דעשר אמירן, בגין דדא כליל עילא ותתא ועל דא כליל בהאי י' זיני דחדוה כו'. ומבאר בהמאמר, שכללא דעשר אמירן כולל עשרת הדברות (עילא) ועשרה מאמרות (תתא), ולהיות שברכה זו היא כללא דעשר אמירן לכן יש בה עשרה לשונות של שמחה (מלבד התיבות חתן וכלה) שהו"ע השמחה בכל ספירה בפרט [שעי"ז דוקא הספירות הן בשלימותן, כמובן מהמבואר בכ"מ[ftnref\_573\_5] עה"פ[ftnref\_574\_6] אם הבנים שמחה]. וממשיך בזהר שם (הובא בהמאמר שם) בענין ברכה השביעית, זכאין אינון ישראל דאינון זכו לתתא כגוונא דלעילא. והיינו[ftnref\_575\_7] שברכה זו היא (גם) שבח והודאה להקב"ה על זה שישראל ע"י עבודתם מבררים ומזככים את המטה (לתתא) שיהי' כגוונא דלעילא, ועי"ז ממשיכים גילוי אוא"ס ב"ה גם בעולמות העליונים (לעילא).
ב) [cup]וביאור[/cup] הענין (וגם השייכות דענין זכו לתתא כגוונא דלעילא לענין חתן וכלה), הוא, דכל הענינים שלמטה משתלשלים מענינם למעלה, ובלשון התניא[ftnref\_576\_8] שנשתלשלו מהן, וכן הוא בחתן וכלה (שלמטה), שהם משתלשלים מחתן וכלה שלמעלה[ftnref\_577\_9], הקב"ה וכנסת ישראל. וכמבואר במדרשי חז"ל שחתן וכלה האמוריםבשה"ש קאי על הקב"ה וכנסת ישראל. וכמרז"ל[ftnref\_578\_10] ביום חתונתו[ftnref\_579\_11] זה מתן תורה, דמתן תורה הו"ע הנישואין דהקב"ה וכנסת ישראל. וי"ל שזהו"ע וטוב לב משתה תמיד[ftnref\_580\_12], כי בכל יום ויום יש הענין דמתן תורה, כמו שאומרים בברכת התורה (שמברכים בכל יום) נותן התורה לשון הוה[ftnref\_581\_13], ועד שבכל יום כל הקורא ושונה הקב"ה קורא ושונה כנגדו[ftnref\_582\_14], ומבאר אדמו"ר הזקן[ftnref\_583\_15] דזה שהקב"ה קורא ושונה כנגדו הוא ממש כמו שהי' בשעת מתן תורה בפעם הראשונה. שלכן אמרו[ftnref\_584\_16] (בנוגע להלכה בפועל) מה להלן באימה וביראה וברתת ובזיע אף כאן באימה וביראה וברתת ובזיע. ובפרט לפי המבואר[ftnref\_585\_17] שד' לשונות אלו (אימה יראה רתת וזיע) הם כנגד ד' האותיות דשם הוי', שבמתן תורה הי' הגילוי דשם הוי' לכ"א מישראל[ftnref\_586\_18], וגילוי זה הוא גם בעסק התורה עכשיו.
[cup]והנה[/cup] ע"י עסק התורה פועלים גם בהעולם שיהי' כלי לאלקות. דע"י שהתורה נסעה וירדה כו' עד שנתלבשה בדברים גשמיים בכדי שיוכל האדם לתפוס אותה במחשבה דיבור ומעשה שלו[ftnref\_587\_19], הנה עי"ז ממשיכים גם בהעולם. דהנה ידוע[ftnref\_588\_20] שעשרה מאמרות שבהם נברא העולם[ftnref\_589\_21] הם מכוונים כנגד עשרת הדברות, כדאיתא בזהר[ftnref\_590\_22] עה"פ[ftnref\_591\_23] עשרה עשרה הכף בשקל הקודש, ולכן ע"י עסק התורה (עשרת הדברות) פועלים בהעשרה מאמרות, שגם הם יהיו בשקל הקודש[ftnref\_592\_24].
[cup]ויש[/cup] לומר, דזה שעשרה מאמרות ועשרת הדברות שקולים זה כנגד זה, הכוונה בזה היא גם שכל אחד פועל בהשני. דכמו שעשרת הדברות פועלים (כנ"ל) בהעשרה מאמרות, וכמאמר[ftnref\_593\_25] אסתכל באורייתא וברא עלמא, שבריאת העולם היא ע"י העשרהמאמרות כמו שהם בתורה[ftnref\_594\_26], שירדו אח"כ בבחי' מילין דהדיוטא[ftnref\_595\_27] להוות את העולם, עד"ז הוא לאידך, שעבודת האדם בעניני העולם שנברא בהעשרה מאמרות מילין דהדיוטא פועלת בעשרת הדברות [שלכן יש פירוש[ftnref\_596\_28] במאמר הזהר עה"פ עשרה עשרה גו', שעשרה מאמרות הם גבוהים מעשרת הדברות]. וי"ל שהוא ע"פ המבואר באגה"ק[ftnref\_597\_29] שבירור הלכה בתורה הוא ע"י נשמה בגוף דוקא, ולכן באים העליונים לשמוע חידושי תורה מהתחתונים (וכמרז"ל[ftnref\_598\_30] עה"פ[ftnref\_599\_31] חברים מקשיבים לקולך). ועד שהקב"ה אומר למלאכי השרת לי מה אתם שואלים אני ואתם נלך אצל ב"ד של מטה[ftnref\_600\_32].
[cup]ועד"ז[/cup] הוא גם בחתן וכלה, דעם היות שהחתן וכלה שלמטה משתלשלים מבחי' חתן וכלה שלמעלה, שענינם בספירות הוא ז"א ומלכות[ftnref\_601\_33], ולמעלה יותר קוב"ה ושכינתי' (חתן וכלה) הו"ע אוא"ס שלפני הצמצום והקו כמבואר בארוכה בדרושי כ"ק אדמו"ר מהר"ש[ftnref\_602\_34], ועד שהענין דקוב"ה ושכינתי' הוא גם לפני הצמצום[ftnref\_603\_35], מ"מ, ע"י העבודה למטה דוקא (ובפרטיות ע"י שמחת חתן וכלה שלמטה) ממשיכים תוספת אור בחתן וכלה שלמעלה גם כמו שהם בשרשם הראשון לפני הצמצום. וע"ד שע"י עסק התורה והמצוות שלמטה דוקא נעשה יחוד קוב"ה ושכינתי'[ftnref\_604\_36] כמבואר בתניא[ftnref\_605\_37], גם כמו שהם למעלה מעלה.
ג) [cup]ומדייק[/cup] בהמאמר[ftnref\_606\_38] למה מקדים ששון ושמחה לפני חתן וכלה, והרי השמחה (שבברכה זו) היא מה שמשמחים את החתן ואת הכלה וכמאמר[ftnref\_607\_39] כיצד מרקדין לפני הכלה. גם אינו מובן[ftnref\_608\_40] מ"ש אשר ברא כו' חתן וכלה, דכיון שהתחלתהענין דחתן וכלה הוא חתן וכלה שלמעלה, איך שייך לומר על זה אשר ברא, לשון בריאה. והרי אפילו כלה (שלמעלה), ספירת המלכות, היא בכלל הספירות דאצילות [עשר ולא תשע[ftnref\_609\_41], כי המלכות משלימה הספירות, אנכי[ftnref\_610\_42] שלומי אמוני ישראל[ftnref\_611\_43]], ועאכו"כ בחתן שלמעלה שהוא ז"א, איך שייך לומר בו הלשון בריאה.
[cup]ומבאר[/cup][ftnref\_612\_44] בהקדם מ"ש[ftnref\_613\_45] לפני לא נוצר א-ל, שאינו מובן[ftnref\_614\_46] איך שייך לשון יצירה על שם א-ל שהוא משמותיו של הקב"ה, מז' השמות שאינם נמחקים[ftnref\_615\_47]. והדיוק בפסוק זה הוא עוד יותר מהדיוק בברכת אשר ברא, כיון שיצירה ("נוצר") היא למטה מבריאה ("ברא").
[cup]והענין[/cup] הוא, שמבאר בהמאמר[ftnref\_612\_44], דשם א-ל שייך לעולמות. דשם א-ל הוא בחסד[ftnref\_616\_48], ובענין החסד כתיב[ftnref\_617\_49] כי אמרתי עולם חסד יבנה, שהכוונה בזה היא שבנין העולם הוא ע"י ספירת החסד ושבנין ספירת החסד הוא בשביל התהוות העולמות[ftnref\_618\_50]. ולכן שייך בשם א-ל לשון יצירה להיותו מקור לעולמות. ועד"ז[ftnref\_619\_51] הוא גם בנוגע לבחי' חתן (ששייך בו לשון בריאה), כי חתן הוא מלשון חות דרגא[ftnref\_620\_52] [דהגם שהחילוק בין ז"א (חתן) למלכות (כלה) הוא שהמלכות גם כמו שהיא באצילות היא השרש ומקור דבי"ע משא"כ ז"א הוא סוף עולמות הא"ס (כמבואר בתו"א[ftnref\_621\_53] ב"כי"ק אדמו"ר נ"ע"), מ"מ חתן הוא מלשון חות דרגא], ירידה, ולכן שייך בו לשון בריאה.
ד) [cup]וביאור[/cup] הענין, הנה ידוע שהסיבה לבריאת העולם וכל סדר ההשתלשלות הוא החסד שבאוא"ס, כמבואר בארוכה בד"ה אתה אחד לאדמו"ר האמצעי[ftnref\_622\_54] ובכמה דרושים שלו. וענין החסד שבאוא"ס הוא כללות ענין הגילוי, וכמו שמבאראדמו"ר הצ"צ ברשימותיו לתהלים[ftnref\_623\_55] עה"פ[ftnref\_624\_56] א-ל אלקים הוי' דיבר ויקרא ארץ, ששמות אלה ישנם גם לפני הצמצום, א-ל (חסד) מורה על התחלת גילוי האור (מה שהי' ממלא מקום החלל[ftnref\_625\_57]). ולכן שם א-ל קדם לשם אלקים, כי תחלה הו"ע הגילוי והוא שרש וסיבת ההתהוות, ולאח"ז (בכדי שיהי' אפשר להיות התהוותם בפועל) הי' הצמצום, אלקים.
[cup]וגם[/cup] בההתהוות עצמה שבה קדם הצמצום וכמרז"ל[ftnref\_626\_58] בתחלה עלה במחשבה לברוא את העולם במדת הדין, הנה נוסף לזה שהצמצום הוא בשביל הגילוי (כמבואר בהמשך תער"ב[ftnref\_627\_59] ובכ"מ), הנה גם הצמצום גופא הוא מצד החסד, וכמו שמבאר הרב המגיד[ftnref\_628\_60] שהצמצום שלמעלה הוא כמו אב המצמצם את שכלו כו' בשביל בנו הקטן כו' האהבה גרמה את הצמצום. היינו שהצמצום הוא בכדי שהאור יומשך ויתקבל בהתחתונים, ועד שיומשך בהם גילוי אור נעלה יותר מהאור שהאיר בגילוי קודם הצמצום[ftnref\_629\_61]. ועד שיהי' דירה לו ית', לו לעצמותו [בדוגמת דירת האדם, שעצמות האדם דר בהדירה][ftnref\_630\_62], ושהעצמות יהי' בהם בגילוי כמ"ש[ftnref\_631\_63] ולא יכנף עוד מוריך.
ה) [cup]והנה[/cup] הסיבה לבנין החסד (בכדי שיהי' התהוות העולם) היא כי חפץ חסד הוא[ftnref\_632\_64]. והענין דחפץ חסד הוא למעלה מחסד (לא רק מחסד כמו שהוא במדות אלא למעלה גם משרשו כמו שהוא במוחין וכו'), כי ענינו הוא חפץ, רצון ותענוג, אלא שהחפץ הוא שיהי' חסד[ftnref\_633\_65]. וחפץ זה הוא הסיבה לבנין החסד שממנו נבנה העולם. ויש לומר, דחתן מלשון חות דרגא הוא בחי' חפץ (חסד) שלמעלה מחסד. דזה שנתעורר ברצון וחפץ שיהי' חסד הוא ירידה (חות דרגא) לגבי עצמות אוא"ס. [ויש לומר דזהו מ"ש בהמאמר[ftnref\_634\_66] שחתן הוא התקשרות, והוא מה שאוא"ס התקשר כביכול לברוא את העולם, דיש לומר, שזה שאוא"ס התקשר כביכול לברוא את העולם הו"ע הרצון והחפץ]. וכיון שהירידה להיות חפץ חסד היא ירידה באין ערוך, לכן שייך בזה לשון בריאה, אשר ברא כו' חתן. ועפ"ז יובן גם מה שבחתן נאמר לשון בריאה, ובשם א-ל נאמר לשון יצירה (לפני לא נוצר א-ל), כי חפץ חסד (שאינו אלא חפץ ורצון שיהי' חסד) הוא למעלה מציור, ולכן שייך בו רק הלשוןבריאה, משא"כ בחסד (שם א-ל בחסד), גם כמו שהוא לפני הצמצום, מכיון שהוא ציור (של חסד), שייך בזה לשון יצירה.
ו) [cup]וזהו[/cup] אשר ברא ששון ושמחה חתן וכלה כו', שקאי (גם) על בריאת חתן וכלה שלמעלה (שמהם משתלשלים חתן וכלה שלמטה). וזה שנקראים בשם חתן וכלה (דשמו אשר יקראו לו בלה"ק מורה על תוכנו של הדבר ועד שהוא המהוה ומחי' ומקיים את הדבר כתורת הבעש"ט הידועה[ftnref\_635\_67]), הוא, כי חתן הוא מלשון חות דרגא כמשנת"ל (סעיף ג), וכלה היא מלשון כליון ומלשון כלתה נפשי[ftnref\_636\_68] [שהם שתי בחי' בכלה, כמבואר בלקו"ת[ftnref\_637\_69]]. והכוונה בבריאת חתן וכלה שלמעלה היא בכדי שיומשכו מהם חתן וכלה שלמטה, וכמבואר לעיל (סעיף ג) בפירוש הכתוב כי אמרתי עולם חסד יבנה שבנין ספירת החסד הוא בכדי שיהי' התהוות העולם. ולכן ע"י עבודת ישראל למטה ובפרט בזה שמשמחים את החתן וכלה (כפשוטם למטה), עי"ז פועלים הן לעילא והן לתתא, ועד שנעשה שמח תשמח רעים האהובים כשמחך יצירך בגן עדן מקדם[ftnref\_638\_70] (קודם החטא) שאז הי' העולם על מילואו[ftnref\_639\_71], אלה תולדות השמים והארץ בהבראם[ftnref\_640\_72], תולדות מלא[ftnref\_641\_73]. ויתירה מזו, דכיון שהכוונה בהירידה (דעולם) שנעשה ע"י חטא עה"ד היא לצורך עלי', הרי מובן שהעלי' היא למקום גבוה יותר מכמו שהי' קודם הירידה, ולכן, ע"י העבודה (שלאחרי החטא) יהי' עילוי נעלה יותר גם מכמו שהי' בתחלת הבריאה, אלה תולדות פרץ[ftnref\_642\_74], תולדות מלא[ftnref\_641\_73], דתולדות מלא שבפסוק זה הוא למעלה יותר מתולדות מלא שבתחלת הבריאה. וגילוי עילוי זה יהי' בביאת משיח צדקנו, כמו שמסיים שם (בתולדות פרץ[ftnref\_643\_75]) וישי הוליד את דוד, דוד מלכא משיחא.
[cup]וגילוי[/cup] זה יהי' בקרוב ממש ובעגלא דידן, בחסד וברחמים ובטוב הנראה והנגלה, ע"י מעשינו ועבודתנו במשך שיתא[ftnref\_644\_76] אלפי שני דהוה עלמא[ftnref\_645\_77] [שזה כולל ענין הזמן (שיתא אלפי שני) וענין המקום (עלמא) יחד], ובפרט ע"י העבודה דיפוצו מעינותיך, המעיינות דתורת הבעש"ט, ובפרט כמו שנתגלו ע"י אדמו"ר הזקן לאחרי י"ט כסלו [וכידוע מאמר אדמו"ר (מהורש"ב) נ"ע[ftnref\_646\_78] שענין יפוצו מעינותיך חוצה הותחל בעיקר לאחרי פטרבורג], שעי"ז אתי מר דא מלכא משיחא[ftnref\_647\_79] שאז יהיוהנישואין של הקב"ה וכנסת ישראל[ftnref\_648\_80], וגם הנישואין דהקב"ה ודכל אחד מישראל, בתכלית השלימות, כמבואר בארוכה בכ"מ[ftnref\_649\_81] עה"פ[ftnref\_650\_82] כימי צאתך מארץ מצרים אראנו נפלאות שהענינים דלע"ל יהיו נפלאות – נו"ן פלאות – אפילו בערך הנסים והנפלאות דיציאת מצרים, שאז יהי' ענין הנישואין בשלימות, בקרוב ממש, במהרה בימינו.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**English:**

## 14 Kislev, 5739

"Who created joy and gladness, bridegroom and bride, mirth, song, delight and rejoicing, love and brotherhood, peace and friendship." My revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, cites in his maamar titled *Zeh* (found in the *Kuntres Drushei Chassuna 5689*), the statement of the Zohar in *Parshas Terumah*, which explains each of the seven *brachos* in detail. Regarding the seventh *bracha*—which includes all the *brachos*—it says: "The seventh is that which sustains everything, and from this seventh everything is blessed; it is certainly the totality of the 'ten utterances,' because it includes both above and below, and therefore it includes these ten types of joy, etc." The maamar explains that the "totality of the ten utterances" includes the Ten Commandments (*ila*—above) and the ten utterances of creation (*tata*—below). Since this *bracha* is the totality of the ten utterances, it contains ten expressions of joy (aside from the words "bridegroom and bride"), which represent the joy in each *sefirah* specifically (for it is precisely through this that the *sefiros* are in their state of perfection, as is understood from what is explained in many places regarding the verse "the mother of children is joyful"). The Zohar continues there (quoted in the maamar) regarding the seventh *bracha*: "Fortunate are the Jewish people, for they have merited to act below in the likeness of above." This means that this *bracha* is also praise and thanksgiving to Hashem for the fact that the Jewish people, through their *avodah*, clarify and refine the lower realm (*tata*) so that it becomes in the likeness of the upper realm (*ila*), and through this, they draw down the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* into the upper worlds as well.

2) The explanation of this matter (and also the connection of "meriting to act below in the likeness of above" to the subject of bridegroom and bride) is that all matters below are derived from their source above—in the language of the *Tanya*, they "are drawn down from them." This is also true of the bridegroom and bride below: they are drawn down from the bridegroom and bride above, Hashem and the *Knesses Yisrael*. As explained in the *Midrashim* of our Sages, the bridegroom and bride mentioned in *Shir HaShirim* refer to Hashem and the *Knesses Yisrael*. As our Sages said, "On the day of His wedding"—this refers to the Giving of the Torah, for the Giving of the Torah is the marriage of Hashem and the *Knesses Yisrael*. It can be said that this is the concept of "a good heart is a constant feast," for every single day there is the event of the Giving of the Torah, as we say in the *bracha* on the Torah (which we recite every day), "Giver of the Torah"—in the present tense. To the extent that every day, "whoever reads and studies, Hashem reads and studies opposite him." The Alter Rebbe explains that this fact—that Hashem reads and studies opposite him—is exactly as it was at the time of the Giving of the Torah the first time. Therefore, they said (regarding the practical *halacha*), "Just as there it was with awe, fear, trembling, and shuddering, so too here it must be with awe, fear, trembling, and shuddering." Especially according to the explanation that these four expressions (awe, fear, trembling, and shuddering) correspond to the four letters of the Name *Havayah*, for at the Giving of the Torah there was the revelation of the Name *Havayah* to every single Jew, and this revelation exists also in the engagement with Torah now.

Now, through engagement with Torah, one also acts upon the world to make it a vessel for *Elokus*. For through the fact that the Torah "traveled and descended, etc.," until it was clothed in physical things so that a person could grasp it in his thought, speech, and action, through this, one draws down into the world as well. It is known that the ten utterances with which the world was created correspond to the Ten Commandments, as it is in the Zohar on the verse "ten, ten, the palm in the holy shekel." Therefore, through engagement with Torah (the Ten Commandments), one acts upon the ten utterances so that they, too, will be in the "holy shekel."

It can be said that the fact that the ten utterances and the Ten Commandments are weighed against one another also means that each acts upon the other. Just as the Ten Commandments act (as mentioned above) upon the ten utterances, and as the statement says, "He looked into the Torah and created the world"—meaning that the creation of the world is through the ten utterances as they are in the Torah, which then descended into the state of "commonplace words" to bring the world into being—so too is it the other way around: the person's *avodah* in the matters of the world, which was created through the ten utterances as "commonplace words," acts upon the Ten Commandments (which is why there is an interpretation in the Zohar on the verse "ten, ten, etc.," that the ten utterances are higher than the Ten Commandments). It can be said that this is according to what is explained in *Iggeres HaKodesh* that the clarification of *halacha* in the Torah is specifically through a soul in a body, and therefore the heavenly beings come to hear *chiddushei Torah* from those below (as our Sages said on the verse "the companions listen to your voice"). To the point that Hashem says to the ministering angels, "What are you asking of Me? I and you shall go to the *Beis Din* below."

Similarly, this is also true regarding the bridegroom and bride. Although the bridegroom and bride below are drawn down from the bridegroom and bride above—whose essence in the *sefiros* is *Ze'er Anpin* and *Malchus*, and higher than that, *Kudsha Brich Hu* and His *Shechinah* (bridegroom and bride) are the *Ohr Ein Sof* before the *Tzimtzum* and the *Kav*, as explained at length in the *drushim* of the Rebbe Maharash, and to the point that the matter of *Kudsha Brich Hu* and His *Shechinah* is even before the *Tzimtzum*—nevertheless, through the *avodah* below specifically (and specifically through the joy of the bridegroom and bride below), one draws down an additional light into the bridegroom and bride above, even as they are in their first root before the *Tzimtzum*. This is similar to how, through the engagement with Torah and *mitzvos* below specifically, the *yichud* of *Kudsha Brich Hu* and His *Shechinah* is accomplished, as explained in the *Tanya*, even as they are in the highest of heights.

3) The maamar notes why it places "joy and gladness" before "bridegroom and bride," when the joy (in this *bracha*) is the fact that one gladdens the bridegroom and the bride, as the statement says, "How does one dance before the bride?" Also, it is not understood why it says "Who created, etc., bridegroom and bride," for since the beginning of the matter of bridegroom and bride is the bridegroom and bride above, how is it possible to say "Who created" (a term of *briah*) regarding this? For even the bride (above), the *sefirah* of *Malchus*, is included in the *sefiros* of *Atzilus* ("ten and not nine," for *Malchus* completes the *sefiros*, "I" [Anochi]—the perfection of the faithful of Israel), and certainly regarding the bridegroom above, who is *Ze'er Anpin*, how can the term *briah* be applied to him?

The maamar explains this by introducing the verse "Before Me no *E-l* was formed," which is difficult: how can the term *yetzirah* ("formed") be applied to *E-l*, which is one of the Names of Hashem, one of the seven Names that may not be erased? The precision in this verse is even greater than the precision in the *bracha* "Who created," since *yetzirah* ("formed") is lower than *briah* ("created").

The matter is that the maamar explains that the Name *E-l* relates to the worlds. For the Name *E-l* is in *Chesed*, and regarding *Chesed* it is written, "For I said, the world shall be built by *Chesed*," which means that the building of the world is through the *sefirah* of *Chesed*, and that the building of the *sefirah* of *Chesed* is for the sake of the coming into being of the worlds. Therefore, the term *yetzirah* is applicable to the Name *E-l* because it is a source for the worlds. Similarly, this is also true regarding the aspect of the bridegroom (to whom the term *briah* is applicable), for "bridegroom" (*chasan*) is from the expression *chut darga* ("descent of level")—for although the difference between *Ze'er Anpin* (bridegroom) and *Malchus* (bride) is that *Malchus*, even as it is in *Atzilus*, is the root and source of *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*, whereas *Ze'er Anpin* is the end of the worlds of *Ein Sof* (as explained in *Torah Or* in the *drushim* of the Rebbe the Tzemach Tzedek), nevertheless, "bridegroom" is from the expression *chut darga*—a descent—and therefore the term *briah* is applicable to him.

4) The explanation of this matter: it is known that the reason for the creation of the world and the entire *seder hishtalshelus* is the *Chesed* that is in the *Ohr Ein Sof*, as explained at length in the *maamar* "Atah Echad" by the Mitteler Rebbe and in many of his *drushim*. The matter of the *Chesed* in the *Ohr Ein Sof* is the general concept of revelation, and as the Tzemach Tzedek explains in his notes to *Tehillim* on the verse "E-l, Elokim, Havayah spoke and called the earth," these Names exist even before the *Tzimtzum*. *E-l* (*Chesed*) indicates the beginning of the revelation of the light (that which filled the space of the void). Therefore, the Name *E-l* precedes the Name *Elokim*, for first is the matter of revelation, which is the root and cause of the coming into being, and afterwards (in order for their coming into being to be possible in actuality) there was the *Tzimtzum*—*Elokim*.

And even in the coming into being itself, in which the *Tzimtzum* preceded, and as our Sages said, "At first it arose in the thought to create the world with the attribute of justice," in addition to the fact that the *Tzimtzum* is for the sake of revelation (as explained in *Hemshech Tza'ar* 5672 and elsewhere), the *Tzimtzum* itself is also from the side of *Chesed*. As the Maggid explains, the *Tzimtzum* above is like a father who constricts his intellect, etc., for the sake of his young son, etc.—the love caused the *Tzimtzum*. That is, the *Tzimtzum* is so that the light may be drawn down and received by those below, to the point that there is drawn down into them a revelation of light even higher than the light that shone in revelation before the *Tzimtzum*. And to the point that there will be a dwelling place for Him, may He be blessed, for His very Essence (like the dwelling of a person, where the essence of the person dwells in the dwelling), and that the Essence will be in them in revelation, as it is written, "Your Teacher shall no longer be hidden."

5) Now, the reason for the building of *Chesed* (so that there would be a coming into being of the world) is because "He desires *Chesed*." The matter of "desiring *Chesed*" is higher than *Chesed* (not only higher than *Chesed* as it is in the *middos*, but higher even than its root as it is in the *mochin*, etc.), for its matter is desire, will, and *taanug*, except that the desire is that there should be *Chesed*. This desire is the cause for the building of the *Chesed* from which the world was built. It can be said that "bridegroom" (*chasan*), from the expression *chut darga*, is the aspect of the "desire (*Chesed*)" that is higher than *Chesed*. For the fact that He was aroused with a will and desire that there should be *Chesed* is a descent (*chut darga*) relative to the Essence of the *Ohr Ein Sof*. (It can be said that this is what is meant in the maamar that "bridegroom" is a connection, and it is that the *Ohr Ein Sof* connected, as it were, to create the world; for it can be said that the fact that the *Ohr Ein Sof* connected, as it were, to create the world is the matter of the will and desire). And since the descent to be a "desire for *Chesed*" is an incomparable descent, therefore the term *briah* is applicable to this: "Who created, etc., bridegroom." Based on this, one can also understand why it is said regarding the bridegroom that it is a term of *briah*, and regarding the Name *E-l* it is said that it is a term of *yetzirah* ("Before Me no *E-l* was formed"), for the "desire for *Chesed*" (which is nothing but a desire and will that there should be *Chesed*) is higher than a configuration (*tziyur*), and therefore only the term *briah* is applicable to it, whereas regarding *Chesed* (the Name *E-l* in *Chesed*), even as it is before the *Tzimtzum*, since it is a configuration (of *Chesed*), the term *yetzirah* is applicable to it.

6) This is "Who created joy and gladness, bridegroom and bride, etc.," which refers (also) to the creation of the bridegroom and bride above (from whom the bridegroom and bride below are drawn down). And the fact that they are called by the name "bridegroom and bride" (for the name by which they are called in the Holy Tongue indicates the content of the thing, to the point that it is what brings into being, sustains, and maintains the thing, according to the well-known teaching of the Baal Shem Tov) is because "bridegroom" is from the expression *chut darga* as explained above (Section 3), and "bride" is from the expression of *kilyon* (longing) and from the expression "my soul longs" (which are two aspects in the bride, as explained in *Likutei Torah*). The intention in the creation of the bridegroom and bride above is so that the bridegroom and bride below should be drawn down from them, as explained above (Section 3) in the interpretation of the verse "For I said, the world shall be built by *Chesed*," that the building of the *sefirah* of *Chesed* is so that there would be a coming into being of the world. Therefore, through the *avodah* of the Jewish people below, and especially in the fact that they gladden the bridegroom and bride (as they are literally below), through this they act both above and below, to the point that it becomes "Gladden the beloved companions as You gladdened Your creation in the Garden of Eden of old" (before the sin), when the world was in its fullness—"These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created" (*behibaram*), generations full. And more than this: since the intention in the descent (of the world) that was caused by the sin of the Tree of Knowledge is for the sake of an ascent, it is understood that the ascent is to a place higher than it was before the descent. Therefore, through the *avodah* (after the sin) there will be an elevation higher even than it was at the beginning of the creation—"These are the generations of Peretz," generations full, for the "generations full" in this verse is higher than the "generations full" at the beginning of the creation. And the revelation of this elevation will be with the coming of *Moshiach Tzidkeinu*, as it concludes there (in the generations of Peretz), "And Yishai begot David," David the King *Moshiach*.

And this revelation will be very soon, in our days, with *Chesed* and mercy, and in manifest and revealed good, through our deeds and our *avodah* during the duration of the six thousand years of the world (which includes the matter of time and the matter of place together), and especially through the *avodah* of "Your springs shall spread forth," the springs of the Torah of the Baal Shem Tov, and especially as they were revealed by the Alter Rebbe after 19 Kislev (and as is known the statement of the Rebbe Rashab that the matter of "Your springs shall spread forth" began primarily after Petersburg), through which "this master, King *Moshiach*, will come," at which time there will be the marriage of Hashem and the *Knesses Yisrael*, and also the marriage of Hashem and every single Jew, in absolute perfection, as explained at length in many places on the verse "As in the days of your exodus from the land of Egypt, I will show him wonders," that the matters of the future will be wonders—*Nun* (50) wonders—even relative to the miracles and wonders of the exodus from Egypt, at which time the matter of the marriage will be in perfection, very soon, speedily in our days.

---

**Hebrew:**

## בס"ד. י"ט כסלו ה'תשל"ט

### בס"ד. י"ט כסלו ה'תשל"ט[ftnref\_651\_\*]

[cup]אמר[/cup] ר' אושעיא מאי דכתיב[ftnref\_652\_1] צדקת פרזונו בישראל גו' צדקה עשה הקב"ה בישראל שפיזרן לבין האומות[ftnref\_653\_2], ומבאר כ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר במאמרו ד"ה זה [שנאמר בי"ט כסלו[ftnref\_654\_3] תרפ"ט בסעודה, ונדפס בקונטרס דרושי חתונה תרפ"ט[ftnref\_655\_4]], דע"י שישראל מפוזרים בכמה מדינות, הנה גם כשבמדינה אחת דוחקים ולוחצים את בנ"י ואין נותנים להם ללמוד תורה ולקיים מצוות, ועד שבכמה מצוות הם אנוסים ממש[ftnref\_656\_5] שאין להם אפשרות כלל לקיים אותם אפילו ע"י מס"נ[ftnref\_657\_6] [ולדוגמא, כשיש גזירת המלכות שלא להכניס להמדינה ד' מינים, שאז גם ע"י מס"נ א"א לקיים המצוה], הנה ע"י שישראל הנמצאים בשאר המדינות עוסקים בתומ"צ, הם נותנים כח ועוז גם לאלו הנמצאים בהמקום שיש שם גזירות, שיוכלו ללמוד תורה ולקיים מצוות. ומן הקל אל הכבד, בתחלה שיוכלו לקיים את המצוות ע"י מס"נ עכ"פ [וכידוע שיש דעה בפוסקים[ftnref\_658\_7] שגם בהמצוות שעליהם אמרו יעבור ואל יהרג רשאי לקדש את השם ולמסור עצמו], ועד שאח"כ לא יצטרכו למס"נ כלל[ftnref\_659\_8], ועד שלא יהי' להם נסיונות ואפילו לא מניעות ועיכובים, כי יצאו לחירות ויוכלו לעסוק בתומ"צ מתוך הרחבה, כמארז"ל[ftnref\_660\_9] כל המקיים את התורה מעוני סופו לקיימה מעושר.
ב) [cup]והנה[/cup] כיון שהתורה היא נצחית בכל עת וזמן ובכל מקום (כמבואר בהמאמר[ftnref\_661\_10]), מובן שצדקה זו היא גם בהמקומות שאין שם גזירות על תומ"צ. ויובן זה ע"פ מ"ש כ"ק מו"ח אדמו"ר (בדרושי חתונה הנ"ל[ftnref\_662\_11]) בענין מצות פדיון שבוייםשישנה גם ברוחניות. דכמו שהשבוי בגשמיות (היושב במאסר) אינו יכול לצאת משם, עד"ז הוא גם ברוחניות, שלפעמים האדם שבוי רח"ל בידי יצרו [וע"ד בעלי עסקים הטרודים ומוטרדים ביותר בטרדות עסקיהם, שהם קשורים (שבויים) בהמחשבות המבלבלות ואינם יכולים לצאת מהם], ועד שלפעמים הוא כמו אנוס[ftnref\_663\_12], ובלשון הרמב"ם[ftnref\_664\_13] (בנוגע להלכה) יצרו הוא שתקפו. וזהו"ע הצדקה ע"י שפיזרן לבין האומות גם בנוגע למקומות הרווחה, דכאשר מי שהוא מישראל שבוי בידי יצרו רח"ל (בנוגע לענין פרטי), יציאתו מהשבי' היא [לא בכח עצמו, כיון שאין חבוש מתיר עצמו[ftnref\_665\_14], כ"א] ע"י יהודי שאינו נתון בשבי' בנוגע לענין זה. והיינו, דגם כאשר גם הפודה אותו מהשבי' הוא שבוי בידי יצרו, ועד שהוא כמו אנוס (יצרו הוא שתקפו) בנוגע למצוה אחרת, מ"מ, כיון שבנוגע לכו"כ מצוות אינו שבוי בידי יצרו, כי התנגדות היצה"ר אצל כ"א היא (בעיקר) בענין אחר [דכמו[ftnref\_666\_15] שבכל זמן יש עבודה מיוחדת השייכת ביותר לזמן זה, ועיקר הפיתויים וההתנגדות של היצה"ר הם למנוע עבודה זו[ftnref\_667\_16], שלכן הנסיונות שבדור אחד שונים מהנסיונות בדורות אחרים [וכמו בזמנו של מנשה שאז הי' שולט היצר דע"ז[ftnref\_668\_17] משא"כ בהדורות שלאחריו, עאכו"כ לאחר שקטלוהו ליצרא דע"ז[ftnref\_669\_18]], עד"ז הוא גם בזמן אחד גופא, שלכל אחד מישראל יש ענין מיוחד (מצוה או מנהג וכיו"ב) שנוגע אליו ביותר, כידוע[ftnref\_670\_19] בביאור מארז"ל[ftnref\_671\_20] אבוך במאי הוה זהיר טפי, ועיקר הפיתויים וההתנגדות של היצה"ר שלו הם בנוגע לענין זה], וע"י שמקיים את המצוות שאין יצרו לוחם כנגדם (כ"כ), הוא פודה מהשבי' את זה שיצרו תקפו במצוות אלו. וזהו צדקה עשה הקב"ה בישראל שפיזרן לבין האומות, דפדיון שבויים הוא צדקה הכי נעלית, וכמבואר במאמר הנ"ל<11> שבמצות צדקה פדיון שבויים הוא קודם לכל דבר ואין מצוה גדולה כפדיון שבויים[ftnref\_672\_21], וע"י *שפיזרן* לבין האומות, שיצרו של אחד שונה מיצרו של השני, עי"ז מתקיימת הצדקה דפדיון שבויים, כנ"ל.ג) [cup]ומדייק[/cup] בהמאמר[ftnref\_673\_22], דלפי פירוש זה בצדקת פרזונו (צדקה כו' שפיזרן כו') הול"ל צדקת פזרונו ולמה נאמר פרזונו. ומבאר[ftnref\_674\_23], שפרזונו הוא ע"ד מ"ש[ftnref\_675\_24] פרזות תשב ירושלים. והיינו, שהצדקה שעשה הקב"ה בישראל בזה שפיזרן לבין האומות הוא בכדי שעי"ז יגיעו למדריגה נעלית יותר מכמו שהיו לפני הירידה דפיזרן, ועד לבחי' פרזות תשב ירושלים (פרזונו).
ד) [cup]ויובן[/cup] זה ע"פ מארז"ל[ftnref\_676\_25] שכל מה שיש בעולם יש באדם [שלכן נקרא האדם עולם קטן[ftnref\_677\_26], וכאו"א חייב לומר בשבילי נברא העולם[ftnref\_678\_27]]. ומזה מובן, שבאדם, אתם קרויין אדם[ftnref\_679\_28], יש בחי' ירושלים. והיינו, שגם בזמן הגלות כשמפני חטאינו גלינו מארצנו ונתרחקנו מעל *אדמתנו*[ftnref\_680\_29] [שענין זה (*גלינו* מארצנו *ונתרחקנו* מעל *אדמתנו*) הוא גם בבנ"י הנמצאים בארץ הקודש תובב"א[ftnref\_681\_30]], מ"מ, ענין ירושלים ברוחניות הוא נצחי גם עכשיו, בכאו"א מישראל. והענין הוא, דהנה ידוע[ftnref\_682\_31] שירושלים פירושו הוא יראה שלם [וענין זה הוא גם בנוגע לעיר ירושלים כפשוטה, כדאיתא בתוספות[ftnref\_683\_32] בשם המדרש[ftnref\_684\_33] ששם ירושלים הוא על שם שלם שקראה מלכי צדק מלך שלם ועל שם יראה שקראה אברהם], שהוא שלימות היראה. וזהו שירושלים הרוחנית היא נצחית גם עכשיו בזמן הגלות, כי בכאו"א מישראל גם בזמן הגלות יש (בהעלם עכ"פ) שלימות היראה, כדלקמן.
[cup]ויובן[/cup] זה בהקדים, דבענינה של ירושלים (שלימות היראה) בעבודת האדם, שני פירושים. דבכ"מ[ftnref\_685\_34] מבואר שירושלים היא היראה כמו שבאה בציור דיראה, וזהו"ע ירושלים של מטה וירושלים של מעלה, כי ביראה יש בכללות שתי מדריגות, יראה תתאה – ירושלים של מטה, ויראה עילאה – ירושלים של מעלה. וזהו אמר הקב"ה לא אבוא בירושלים של מעלה עד שאבוא לירושלים של מטה[ftnref\_686\_35], דהגם שמצדהגילויים יראה תתאה היא למטה מטה לגבי יראה עילאה, מ"מ, כיון שיראה תתאה שרשה מיראה עילאה[ftnref\_687\_36], ועד ששניהם חד הוא[ftnref\_688\_37], לכן, ע"י יראה תתאה (ירושלים של מטה) מגיעים ליראה עילאה (ירושלים של מעלה)[ftnref\_689\_38]. ומהמשך הענינים שבמאמר זה מובן[ftnref\_690\_39], דירושלים הוא נקודת היראת שמים שלמעלה מהשכל, נקודה שלמעלה מציור, שישנה בכאו"א מישראל, מצד עצם הנשמה שבו שלמעלה מגילויים[ftnref\_691\_40]. ויש לומר, דמהטעמים על זה שנקודה זו נקראת בשם ירושלים, שלימות היראה, הוא, כי אמיתית ענין השלימות הוא *שאין שייך* שיהי' בו חסרון. ולכן, היראה כמו שבאה בגילוי (בציור דיראה), כיון שאפשר שיהי' בה חסרון, אין בה אמיתית השלימות, ואמיתית השלימות היא בנקודת היראת שמים שלמעלה מגילוי, דנקודה זו ישנה תמיד בכאו"א מישראל.
ה) [cup]והנה[/cup] אף שנקודת היראת שמים שלמעלה מהשכל היא למעלה מהגבלה, מ"מ, יש בה (בהתגלותה) כו"כ מדריגות, ואמיתית הבל"ג שבה יהי' לעתיד לבוא. וזהו שלע"ל (דוקא) יהי' פרזות תשב ירושלים, ופי' רש"י[ftnref\_692\_41] שלא יהא לה קצב ושיעור כו'. וגם אז יהיו בה עליות לאין קץ, כמ"ש רש"י[ftnref\_692\_41] שתגדל מיום ליום. וזהו צדקת פרזונו בישראל כו' צדקה עשה הקב"ה בישראל שפיזרן לבין האומות, דזה שנאמר בכתוב ענין זה (צדקה כו' שפיזרן כו') בלשון פרזונו הוא, כי הצדקה שעשה הקב"ה בישראל ע"י שפיזרן לבין האומות הוא שעי"ז יגיעו לפרזונו שלמעלה ממדידה והגבלה. ושני ענינים בזה. שע"י המניעות ועכובים שבזמן הגלות (פיזרן לבין האומות) מתעוררת ומתגלה נקודת היראת שמים שבכל אחד מישראל (כמבואר בארוכה בהמאמר[ftnref\_693\_42]) שהיא למעלה ממדידה והגבלה. ויתירה מזו, דענין פרזונו רומז (כמובא לעיל סעיף ג מהמאמר) על פרזות תשב ירושלים, שע"י התעוררות נקודת היראת שמים בזמן הגלות, תהי' אח"כ הוספה ועלי' בנקודת היראת שמים, הבל"ג שבה כמו שיהי' לע"ל.
ו) [cup]ויש[/cup] לומר, דבפרזות תשב ירושלים (נקודת היראת שמים) שני ענינים. שנקודת היראת שמים עצמה היא למעלה מהגבלה (כנ"ל סעיף ה), וגם התפשטותה[ftnref\_694\_43] היא בלי גבול. ובזה גופא שני ענינים. שנקודת היראת שמים פועלת בכל כחות הנפששיהיו בהתאם להנקודה, ושגם הנקודה עצמה מתפשטת בכל כחות הנפש[ftnref\_695\_44], עד בהלבושים דמחשבה דיבור ומעשה. ובדוגמת פרזות תשב ירושלים כפשוטה, דנוסף לזה שירושלים היא נקודה דאמצעו של העולם ומאבן שתי' שבבית המקדש הושתת כל העולם[ftnref\_696\_45], הנה גם ירושלים עצמה תתפשט בכל העולם, דעתידה ירושלים שתתפשט בכל ארץ ישראל ועתידה ארץ ישראל שתתפשט בכל הארצות[ftnref\_697\_46].
ז) [cup]ולכאורה[/cup] יש להוסיף, דזה שנאמר פרזונו (ולא פזרונו), ענין פרזות תשב ירושלים, שייך גם להצדקה שפיזרן שמבאר בתחלת המאמר, שעסק התומ"צ של ישראל הנמצאים במקומות הרווחה נותן כח ועוז לישראל הנמצאים במקום הגזירה שיוכלו ללמוד תורה ולקיים מצוות. כי זה שעסק התומ"צ של אחד נותן כח ועוז לשני הוא לפי שכל ישראל הם מציאות אחת, בפרט מצד נקודת היראת שמים והביטול שבהם, שהיא נקודה המאחדת את כל ישראל. ולכן, בכדי שהעסק דתומ"צ של אחד יתן כח ועוז לאחר (ענין הצדקה בזה שפיזרן) הוא כשעסק התומ"צ שלו הוא בהנקודה דיראת שמים, פרזות תשב ירושלים, שהיראת שמים שלו (ירושלים) היא באופן דופרצת[ftnref\_698\_47] ימה וקדמה וצפונה ונגבה, שמתפשטת וחודרת בכל כחות הנפש, עד במחשבה דיבור ומעשה שלו.
[cup]ויש[/cup] לומר שזהו גם הטעם על זה שהעסק בתומ"צ של אחד נותן כח ועוז (לא רק לאלה הנמצאים במקום הגזירה, אלא) גם לאלו השבויים בידי יצרם, לפדות אותם מהשבי' (כנ"ל סעיף ב), כי בכאו"א מישראל, אפילו ברשעים, יש (בהעלם עכ"פ) נקודת היראת שמים, ולכן כל הסוגים דישראל (צדיקים בינונים ורשעים) הם מציאות אחת. וכמרומז גם בתיבת צבור (שישראל הם צבור[ftnref\_699\_48]), צבור ר"ת *צ*דיקים *ב*ינונים *ור*שעים[ftnref\_700\_49], שכל ג' סוגים אלו הם מציאות אחת [והוא"ו (שבתיבת צבור) הוא וא"ו המחבר את הרשעים עם הבינונים והצדיקים, ועד שהראש של כל התיבה הוא אות צדי"ק, ועמך[ftnref\_701\_50] כולם צדיקים]. ולכן עסק התומ"צ של הצדיקים והבינונים [ובפרט כשעסק התומ"צ שלהם הוא באופן שהם כוללים עצמם עם כל ישראל[ftnref\_702\_51], ואהבת לרעך כמוך[ftnref\_703\_52]] פועל גם על אלו השבויים בידי יצרם, שיצאו מהשבי' ויהיו צדיקים (גם בגלוי).ח) [cup]והנה[/cup] ענין הנ"ל (שהעסק דתומ"צ של אחד נותן כח ועוז גם לשני) הוא לא רק כשהאונס (בנוגע לאיזו מצוה) הוא מפני גזירת המלכות או מפני שיצרו הוא שתקפו, אלא גם כשהאונס הוא מפני שע"פ תורה א"א לקיים מצוה זו, וכמו ישראל הנמצאים בחו"ל שע"פ תורה א"א להם לקיים את המצוות התלויות בארץ, ועד"ז בכללות זמן הגלות שא"א (גם לבנ"י הנמצאים בא"י) לקיים מצוות התלויות בזמן שביהמ"ק הי' קיים, הנה מצד זה שכל ישראל שבכל המקומות ושבכל הזמנים הם מציאות אחת, לכן עי"ז שבנ"י הנמצאים בא"י מקיימים את המצוות התלויות בארץ, מועיל זה גם עבור בנ"י הנמצאים בחו"ל, ועד"ז הוא בנוגע להמצוות התלויות בזמן הבית שקיימו ישראל אז, שזה מועיל גם לבנ"י שבזמן הגלות, כיון שכל ישראל שבמשך כל הדורות הם צבור אחד דאין מיתה בצבור[ftnref\_704\_53] [שלכן הקרבן שהקריבו בימי עזרא כיפר גם על ישראל שהיו בדורו של צדקיהו[ftnref\_705\_54]].
ט) [cup]וזהו[/cup] צדקת פרזונו בישראל צדקה כו' שפיזרן לבין האומות, דזה שנאמר פרזונו (ולא פזרונו) הוא, כי צדקה שעשה הקב"ה בישראל שפיזרן לבין האומות הוא שעי"ז יגיעו לע"ל לפרזות תשב ירושלים (כנ"ל בארוכה). ועוד טעם על זה שנאמר פרזונו הוא, כי בכדי לבוא לפרזות תשב ירושלים הוא ע"י שגם העבודה בזמן הגלות (כשפיזרן לבין האומות) היא באופן דפרזות תשב ירושלים. דנוסף לזה שהכניסה לירושלים בגשמיות, בגאולה האמיתית ע"י משיח צדקנו, היא ע"י העבודה דירושלים, שלימות היראה [כי ביאת המשיח תהי' ע"י בקולו תשמעו[ftnref\_706\_55], שזה (ענין בקולו תשמעו) הוא ע"י היראה, שהיא ראשית העבודה ועיקרה ושרשה[ftnref\_707\_56], דהגם שבכדי שקיום התומ"צ יהי' בשלימות צריך גם לאהבה[ftnref\_708\_57], הרי עיקר העבודה היא היראה[ftnref\_709\_58]], הנה בכדי שיהי' פרזות תשב ירושלים, צריכה להיות העבודה גם עכשיו באופן של פרזות, למעלה ממדידה והגבלה, שבכללות היא העבודה דמסירת נפש, מסירת הרצון[ftnref\_710\_59], שעבודה זו היא הכנה קרובה שיהי' פרזות תשב ירושלים גם כפשוטו, עתידה ירושלים שתתפשט בכל ארץ ישראל ועתידה ארץ ישראל שתתפשט בכל הארצות. שזה נעשה (ככל הענינים דלעתיד[ftnref\_711\_60]) ע"י מעשינו ועבודתנו במשך זמן הגלות, שכללותם הם הג' קוין דתורה עבודה וגמ"ח [וכמארז"ל במסכת ברכות בתחלתה ובראשיתה[ftnref\_712\_61] עה"פ[ftnref\_713\_62]פדה בשלום נפשי מקרב לי כי ברבים היו עמדי, כל העוסק בתורה ובגמ"ח ומתפלל עם הצבור מעלה אני עליו כאילו פדאני לי ולבני מבין אומות העולם], ובפרט ע"י העבודה דיפוצו מעינותיך חוצה שעי"ז אתי מר דא מלכא משיחא [כמ"ש באגה"ק של הבעש"ט[ftnref\_714\_63] מה ששמע בהיכל המשיח, ממלכא משיחא].
[cup]וזהו[/cup] צדקה כו' שפיזרן לבין האומות, שע"י מעשינו ועבודתנו בזמן הגלות (בג' הקוין דתורה עבודה וגמ"ח ובפרט בהפצת המעינות חוצה) שבכל יום ויום, הנה נוסף על זה שבכל יום ויום (כשנמצאים עדיין בגלות) נעשה הפדי' ברוחניות (*כאילו* פדאני), הנה כל פעולה ופעולה בהנ"ל מצטרפת לחשבון גדול של הצדקה[ftnref\_715\_64] שמקרבת את הגאולה[ftnref\_716\_65] כפשוטה בגשמיות, שאז יהי' פרזות תשב ירושלים גם בגשמיות, בביאת משיח צדקנו, בקרוב ממש.
\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

**English:**

## 19 Kislev 5739

Rabbi Oshaya said: What is the meaning of the verse, "The righteousness of His open cities in Israel..."? It means that Hashem performed an act of righteousness for Israel by scattering them among the nations. The Rebbe Rayatz explains in his maamar titled *Zeh* [delivered on 19 Kislev 5729 at the farbrengen, and printed in *Kuntres Dirushei Chassunah 5729*] that because the Jewish people are scattered across many lands, even when they are oppressed and pressured in one country—to the point where they are forbidden from studying Torah and observing mitzvos, and are truly coerced [in a situation where it is impossible to observe them even with mesiras nefesh, for example, when there is a government decree forbidding the entry of the Arba Minim into the country]—the fact that other Jews in other lands are engaged in Torah and mitzvos provides strength and fortitude to those in the place of decrees, enabling them to study Torah and observe mitzvos. This proceeds from the easier to the more difficult: first, that they may observe the mitzvos at least through mesiras nefesh [as is known, there is an opinion among the poskim that even regarding mitzvos for which the rule is "transgress and do not be killed," one is permitted to sanctify Hashem’s name and sacrifice oneself], and eventually, that they will not need mesiras nefesh at all, and ultimately, that they will have no trials, nor even obstacles and delays, for they will emerge into freedom and be able to engage in Torah and mitzvos with expansion, as our Sages said: "Whoever observes the Torah out of poverty will eventually observe it out of wealth."

2) Since the Torah is eternal at every time, in every era, and in every place (as explained in the maamar), it is understood that this "righteousness" applies even in places where there are no decrees against Torah and mitzvos. This can be understood based on what the Rebbe Rayatz said (in the aforementioned *Dirushei Chassunah*) regarding the mitzvah of redeeming captives, which also exists in a spiritual sense. Just as a captive in the physical sense (who sits in prison) cannot leave, so too is it in the spiritual realm; sometimes a person is a captive, Heaven forbid, in the hands of his yetzer [similar to business people who are extremely preoccupied and troubled by the concerns of their business, such that they are bound (captive) in confusing thoughts and cannot escape them], to the point that he is sometimes like one coerced, or in the language of the Rambam (regarding halacha), his yetzer has overpowered him. This is the concept of the "righteousness" of scattering them among the nations, even regarding places of prosperity: when a Jew is captive in the hands of his yetzer (regarding a specific matter), his release from captivity is [not by his own power, for a prisoner cannot free himself, but rather] through a Jew who is not in captivity regarding that specific matter. This means that even if the one who redeems him from captivity is himself a captive in the hands of his yetzer—and is even like one coerced (his yetzer has overpowered him) regarding a different mitzvah—nevertheless, since regarding many other mitzvos he is not captive, because the opposition of the yetzer hara for each person is (primarily) in a different area [just as in every time there is a specific avodah that belongs most to that time, and the main temptations and opposition of the yetzer hara are to prevent this avodah, which is why the trials of one generation differ from the trials of other generations (like in the time of Menashe, when the yetzer for avodah zarah was dominant, unlike in the generations that followed, and certainly after they killed the yetzer for avodah zarah), so too is it within one time itself; every Jew has a specific matter (a mitzvah or custom, etc.) that concerns him most, as is known in the explanation of the Sages' words, "In what was your father most careful?" and the main temptations and opposition of his yetzer hara are regarding this matter], by observing the mitzvos against which his yetzer does not fight (as much), he redeems from captivity the one whose yetzer has overpowered him in those mitzvos. This is the "righteousness Hashem performed for Israel by scattering them among the nations," for the redemption of captives is the most exalted form of righteousness, and as explained in the aforementioned maamar, regarding the mitzvah of tzedakah, the redemption of captives takes precedence over everything, and there is no mitzvah as great as the redemption of captives. By *scattering* them among the nations, such that the yetzer of one is different from the yetzer of another, the righteousness of redeeming captives is fulfilled, as stated above.

3) The maamar notes that according to this interpretation of "the righteousness of His open cities" (*tzidkas parzono*—righteousness, etc., that He scattered them, etc.), it should have said *tzidkas pizarono* (the righteousness of His scattering); why is it said *parzono*? It explains that *parzono* is related to the verse, "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities (*perazos*)." This means that the righteousness Hashem performed for Israel by scattering them among the nations is so that through this they will reach a more exalted level than they were at before the descent of being scattered, reaching the level of "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities" (*parzono*).

4) This can be understood based on the words of our Sages that everything that exists in the world exists in man [which is why man is called a "small world," and everyone is obligated to say, "The world was created for my sake"]. From this it is understood that in man—"you are called 'man'"—there is the level of Jerusalem. This means that even in the time of exile, when because of our sins we were exiled from our land and distanced from our *soil* [a matter that (being exiled from our land and distanced from our soil) applies even to Bnei Yisrael who are in the Holy Land], the concept of Jerusalem in the spiritual sense is eternal even now, in every Jew. The matter is that it is known that Jerusalem (*Yerushalayim*) means "complete awe" (*yirah shalem*) [a concept that also applies to the city of Jerusalem literally, as is found in Tosafos in the name of the Midrash that the name Jerusalem is on account of *Shalem*, as it was called by Malki Tzedek, King of Shalem, and on account of *Yirah*, as it was called by Avraham], which is the perfection of awe. This is why spiritual Jerusalem is eternal even now in the time of exile, because in every Jew, even in the time of exile, there is (at least in concealment) the perfection of awe, as follows.

This can be understood by prefacing that regarding Jerusalem (the perfection of awe) in man’s avodah, there are two interpretations. In many places it is explained that Jerusalem is awe as it comes in the form of a "picture" of awe; this is the concept of Jerusalem below and Jerusalem above, for in awe there are generally two levels: *yirah tata'ah* (lower awe)—Jerusalem below, and *yirah ila'ah* (higher awe)—Jerusalem above. This is what Hashem said: "I will not enter the Jerusalem above until I enter the Jerusalem below," for even though in terms of revelations *yirah tata'ah* is far below *yirah ila'ah*, nevertheless, since *yirah tata'ah* has its root in *yirah ila'ah*, and they are both one, therefore, through *yirah tata'ah* (Jerusalem below) one reaches *yirah ila'ah* (Jerusalem above). From the continuation of the matters in this maamar, it is understood that Jerusalem is the point of *yiras shamayim* that is above intellect, a point above any "picture," which exists in every Jew by virtue of the essence of the neshamah within him, which is above revelations. It can be said that one of the reasons this point is called Jerusalem—the perfection of awe—is because the true meaning of perfection is that *it is impossible* for there to be a lack in it. Therefore, awe as it comes in revelation (in a "picture" of awe), since it is possible for there to be a lack in it, does not possess true perfection; true perfection is in the point of *yiras shamayim* that is above revelation, and this point exists always in every Jew.

5) Now, although the point of *yiras shamayim* that is above intellect is above limitation, nevertheless, it has (in its revelation) many levels, and the true infinity within it will be in the future to come. This is why only in the future will "Jerusalem be inhabited as open cities," and Rashi explains that it will have no limit or measure, etc. Even then, there will be infinite ascents within it, as Rashi says that it will grow from day to day. This is "the righteousness of His open cities in Israel, etc.—the righteousness Hashem performed for Israel by scattering them among the nations," for the reason this matter is stated in the verse (righteousness, etc., that He scattered them, etc.) using the term *parzono* is because the righteousness Hashem performed for Israel by scattering them among the nations is so that through this they will reach the *parzono* that is above measurement and limitation. There are two aspects to this: through the obstacles and delays in the time of exile (scattering them among the nations), the point of *yiras shamayim* in every Jew is awakened and revealed (as explained at length in the maamar), which is above measurement and limitation. Furthermore, the concept of *parzono* hints (as mentioned above in section 3 of the maamar) to "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities," that through the awakening of the point of *yiras shamayim* during the time of exile, there will subsequently be an addition and ascent in the point of *yiras shamayim*, the infinity within it, as it will be in the future to come.

6) It can be said that in "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities" (the point of *yiras shamayim*) there are two matters: that the point of *yiras shamayim* itself is above limitation (as in section 5), and also that its expansion is without limit. Within this itself there are two matters: that the point of *yiras shamayim* acts upon all the powers of the soul so that they are in accordance with that point, and that the point itself expands into all the powers of the soul, down to the garments of thought, speech, and action. This is analogous to "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities" literally, for in addition to Jerusalem being the point of the center of the world, and that from the *Even Shetiyah* in the Beis Hamikdash the entire world was founded, Jerusalem itself will expand into the entire world, for Jerusalem is destined to expand into the entire Land of Israel, and the Land of Israel is destined to expand into all the lands.

7) Seemingly, one should add that the fact that it says *parzono* (and not *pizarono*), the concept of "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities," also relates to the righteousness of scattering them that is explained at the beginning of the maamar: that the engagement in Torah and mitzvos of the Jews in places of prosperity gives strength and fortitude to the Jews in the place of decrees so that they can study Torah and observe mitzvos. This is because the fact that the engagement in Torah and mitzvos of one gives strength and fortitude to another is because all Jews are one entity, especially by virtue of the point of *yiras shamayim* and the *bitul* within them, which is the point that unites all of Israel. Therefore, in order for the engagement in Torah and mitzvos of one to give strength and fortitude to another (the concept of righteousness in scattering them), it must be that his engagement in Torah and mitzvos is in the point of *yiras shamayim*, "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities," where his *yiras shamayim* (Jerusalem) is in a manner of "spreading out" to the west, east, north, and south, such that it expands and permeates all the powers of the soul, down to his thought, speech, and action.

It can be said that this is also the reason why the engagement in Torah and mitzvos of one gives strength and fortitude (not only to those in the place of the decree, but) also to those who are captive in the hands of their yetzer, to redeem them from captivity (as in section 2), because in every Jew, even in the wicked, there is (at least in concealment) the point of *yiras shamayim*, and therefore all types of Jews (tzaddikim, beinonim, and resha'im) are one entity. This is also hinted at in the word *tzibur* (that Israel is a *tzibur*), for *tzibur* is an acronym for *tzaddikim*, *beinonim*, and *resha'im*, that all three types are one entity [and the *vav* (in the word *tzibur*) is the *vav* that connects the *resha'im* with the *beinonim* and the *tzaddikim*, and the head of the entire word is the letter *tzadik*, and "Your people are all righteous"]. Therefore, the engagement in Torah and mitzvos of the *tzaddikim* and *beinonim* [especially when their engagement in Torah and mitzvos is in a manner where they include themselves with all of Israel, "love your neighbor as yourself"] also acts upon those who are captive in the hands of their yetzer, so that they will emerge from captivity and become *tzaddikim* (even in revelation).

8) The aforementioned matter (that the engagement in Torah and mitzvos of one gives strength and fortitude also to another) is not only when the coercion (regarding some mitzvah) is due to a government decree or because his yetzer has overpowered him, but even when the coercion is because according to Torah it is impossible to observe this mitzvah—like Jews living in the Diaspora who, according to Torah, cannot observe the mitzvos dependent on the Land, and similarly regarding the entire time of exile, when it is impossible (even for Bnei Yisrael in the Holy Land) to observe the mitzvos dependent on the time when the Beis Hamikdash was standing. By virtue of the fact that all Jews in all places and at all times are one entity, therefore, through the fact that Bnei Yisrael who are in the Holy Land observe the mitzvos dependent on the Land, this is effective also for Bnei Yisrael who are in the Diaspora. The same applies to the mitzvos dependent on the time of the Beis Hamikdash that the Jews observed then; this is effective also for Bnei Yisrael in the time of exile, because all Jews throughout all generations are one *tzibur*, and "there is no death in a *tzibur*" [which is why the sacrifice offered in the days of Ezra atoned also for the Jews who were in the generation of Tzidkiyahu].

9) This is "the righteousness of His open cities in Israel—the righteousness Hashem performed for Israel by scattering them among the nations." The reason it says *parzono* (and not *pizarono*) is because the righteousness Hashem performed for Israel by scattering them among the nations is so that through this they will reach, in the future, "Jerusalem shall be inhabited as open cities" (as explained at length above).
